title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new city museum of science.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally took the kids to the new 'Discovery Centre'. They loved the robot lab. || Woman: [1]I found the entrance fee a bit hard to justify[/1], to be honest. It’s nearly double what the old museum used to charge. || Man: It is pricey. But I suppose you’re paying for the high-tech exhibits. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the clarity of the explanations[/2]. Even the really complex physics concepts were broken down into language a six-year-old could grasp. || Man: I agree. It didn't feel like they were talking down to the visitors. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great educational resource, if you can afford to get through the door.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally took the kids to the new 'Discovery Centre'. They loved the robot lab.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the entrance fee a bit hard to justify, to be honest. It’s nearly double what the old museum used to charge.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is pricey. But I suppose you’re paying for the high-tech exhibits.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the clarity of the explanations. Even the really complex physics concepts were broken down into language a six-year-old could grasp.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It didn't feel like they were talking down to the visitors.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great educational resource, if you can afford to get through the door.

questions.question: What does the woman find disappointing about the museum?
questions.explanation: She finds the entrance fee 'hard to justify' and mentions it's double the old price [1].

questions.question: What did the woman value most about the exhibits?

options.A: the advanced level of the technology
options.B: the accessible nature of the information
options.C: the variety of the topics covered

questions.explanation: She praises the 'clarity of the explanations' for complex physics [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a change in their company's email policy.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, no more internal emails on Fridays. Management wants us to 'talk more'. What's your take? || Woman: [3]I suspect it will actually lead to more frequent and unnecessary meetings[/3], which is the last thing we need. || Man: That's a worry. I usually use Friday to clear my inbox for the following week. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that people are already starting to use instant messaging apps instead[/4], so the volume of digital noise hasn't really changed. || Man: I agree. It’s just shifted from one platform to another. || Woman: Exactly. If they wanted to reduce distractions, they should have just told us to stop copying everyone into every single message.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, no more internal emails on Fridays. Management wants us to 'talk more'. What's your take?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect it will actually lead to more frequent and unnecessary meetings, which is the last thing we need.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's a worry. I usually use Friday to clear my inbox for the following week.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that people are already starting to use instant messaging apps instead, so the volume of digital noise hasn't really changed.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s just shifted from one platform to another.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they wanted to reduce distractions, they should have just told us to stop copying everyone into every single message.

questions.question: The woman believes the new policy will result in

options.B: an increase in alternative forms of disruption.
options.C: a decline in the quality of team communication.

questions.explanation: She fears it will lead to 'more frequent and unnecessary meetings' [3].

questions.question: What has the woman observed since the policy was introduced?

options.A: staff being more collaborative in person
options.B: a transition to different communication tools
options.C: a reduction in the time spent on digital tasks

questions.explanation: She notes people are using 'instant messaging apps instead' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new local park that has recently opened.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I took the dog to the new 'West-Side Park' this morning. It’s great to have some green space so close to the city. || Woman: [5]I found the lack of shaded areas a bit problematic[/5], especially in this heat. There are hardly any mature trees. || Man: I agree, it’s a bit exposed. I suppose it’ll take ten years for the new ones to grow. || Woman: [6]I did think the playground equipment was exceptionally well-designed, though[/6]. It’s much safer than the old wooden structures in the town centre. || Man: I agree, it’s very modern. I just wish they’d included a small café area so parents could get a coffee while the kids play. || Woman: That would have been a nice touch, but at least there’s a water fountain.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I took the dog to the new 'West-Side Park' this morning. It’s great to have some green space so close to the city.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the lack of shaded areas a bit problematic, especially in this heat. There are hardly any mature trees.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it’s a bit exposed. I suppose it’ll take ten years for the new ones to grow.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did think the playground equipment was exceptionally well-designed, though. It’s much safer than the old wooden structures in the town centre.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it’s very modern. I just wish they’d included a small café area so parents could get a coffee while the kids play.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That would have been a nice touch, but at least there’s a water fountain.

questions.question: What is the woman's main criticism of the park?

options.A: the distance from the local housing
options.B: the inadequate protection from the sun
options.C: the poor quality of the walking paths

questions.explanation: She complains about the 'lack of shaded areas' and the absence of mature trees [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the park's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'well-designed' and 'safe' playground equipment [6].
